name: Cranberry Quince Jelly
recipeInstructions:
1. Wash the quinces and remove stems and blossom ends. Chop with a coarse knife or food chopper.
2. Combine chopped quinces with stemmed, washed cranberries.
3. Add water to the mixture.
4. Cook until the fruit is soft.
5. Pour into a jelly bag and drain overnight.
6. Empty juice from the jelly bag into a large saucepan.
7. Boil hard for 20 minutes.
8. Measure the hot sugar and add an equal amount of sugar.
9. Continue boiling rapidly until the jelly sets when a spoon is lifted out.
Florence Taft Eaton, Concord, MA.
